commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4cca3ab161875187b19ef50bb99a937d31d8a068)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads channel/account context into image-tool media-root resolution, adds current channel inbound attachment roots to the shared media local-root allowlist, and adds focused tests plus a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +106, Docs +1. Total +139 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: iMessage returns a wildcard default attachment root, while the image-tool localRoots guard treats roots as literal filesystem directories. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the PR has a P1 wildcard-root blocker, missing contributor real behavior proof, and a protected maintainer label; automation cannot supply the contributor's iMessage proof.

target.title: Fix iMessage image attachment roots
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the remaining current-main iMessage image-tool attachment-root bug from #30170 by allowing media analysis to resolve the current channel/account inbound attachment roots through the existing public channel media contract.

This replaces the stale/conflicting #39034 approach with a narrow current-main fix. Thanks @vincentkoc for identifying and proving the original media-root regression.

## Behavior

Before this change, the image tool built local media roots from the generic defaults and workspace only, so valid iMessage attachment paths configured for the current account were rejected as `path-not-allowed` even while iMessage monitoring could read those same roots.

After this change, the image tool passes its current channel/account context into the shared media-root resolver. The resolver only asks the channel media contract for inbound attachment roots when channel context is present, so default local roots are not globally widened.

Fixes #30170.
Refs #39034.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/channel-inbound-roots.fast-path.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-tool-shared.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts` - 5 files, 176 tests passed
- `pnpm check:changed` - passed
- `git diff --check` - passed

## Risk

The change touches shared media-root resolution, but the new roots are gated by explicit channel context and account id. Existing workspace-only policy still short-circuits before channel roots are added.
target.author: omarshahine

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
